questions.explanation: Copper is a good conductor of electricity, while rubber, plastic, and wood are insulators, as per the Chemistry in Everyday Life unit.

questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil, as taught in The World of Plants unit.

options: A magnet has two poles: North and South.
A magnet can be used to make a compass.
questions.explanation: A magnet has two poles (North and South), and like poles repel, not attract. Magnets attract only certain metals like iron. They are used in compasses, but the correct statement is about poles.

questions.question: What is the main function of the stem in a plant?
options: To support the plant and transport nutrients
questions.explanation: The stem supports the plant and transports water and nutrients, as per The World of Plants unit.

questions.explanation: A cow is a herbivore as it eats only plants, from The World of Animals unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a natural fibre?
questions.explanation: Cotton is a natural fibre obtained from plants, as taught in the Chemistry in Everyday Life unit.

questions.explanation: According to Newton's first law of motion, an object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by an external force.

questions.explanation: Frictional force acts when two surfaces are in contact, while magnetic, gravitational, and electrostatic forces are non-contact forces.

questions.question: Which of the following is an element?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is an element; water, carbon dioxide, and salt are compounds.

questions.question: Which part of the plant absorbs water and minerals from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil.

questions.explanation: Chickenpox is caused by a virus and spreads from person to person; diabetes, cancer, and asthma are non-communicable.

questions.explanation: Fishes are aquatic vertebrates with scales and fins.

questions.explanation: Friction is a force that opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact.

questions.explanation: The SI unit of force is newton (N).

options: A is true, B is false
A is false, B is true
Both A and B are true
Both A and B are false
questions.explanation: Washing hands before eating prevents germs from entering the body. Covering the mouth while coughing prevents the spread of germs. So, A is true and B is false. This is from 'Health and Hygiene'.

options: A is true, B is false
A is false, B is true
Both A and B are true
Both A and B are false
questions.explanation: A magnet has two poles: North and South. Like poles (North-North or South-South) repel each other, while unlike poles attract. So, A is true and B is false. This is from the 'Magnetism' unit.

questions.explanation: Light always travels in a straight line, which is the principle of rectilinear propagation.

questions.question: Which organ in the human body pumps blood?
questions.explanation: The heart is the muscular organ that pumps blood throughout the body.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about magnets is true?
questions.explanation: Every magnet has two poles: north and south. Like poles repel, unlike poles attract. Magnets can be natural or artificial and can lose strength.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced in the skin when exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Sunlight helps the skin produce Vitamin D, which is essential for calcium absorption.

questions.question: True or False: Plants take in carbon dioxide through stomata for photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: Stomata are openings on leaves that allow carbon dioxide to enter for photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mixture?
questions.explanation: Air is a mixture of gases like nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, etc. Pure water, iron, and oxygen are pure substances.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a source of water?
questions.explanation: Desert is not a source of water; it is a dry region. Rain, rivers and wells are sources of water.

questions.question: Which of the following is a push or pull acting on an object?
questions.explanation: Force is defined as a push or pull that changes or tends to change the state of rest or motion of an object, as per the Force and Motion unit.

questions.question: True or False: The human heart has four chambers.
questions.explanation: The human heart has four chambers: two atria and two ventricles, as taught in the Health and Hygiene unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a sense organ in humans?
questions.explanation: Skin is a sense organ that detects touch, pressure, and temperature, as per the World of Animals unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a renewable source of energy?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable, while coal, petroleum, and natural gas are non-renewable, as per the Our Environment unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the stem in plants?
questions.explanation: The stem transports water, minerals, and food between roots and leaves, as per The World of Plants unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a sense organ?
questions.explanation: Eyes are sense organs that help us to see. The other options are internal organs.

questions.question: Statement A: The skeletal system helps in movement. Statement B: The skeletal system produces blood cells. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and B are correct
questions.explanation: The skeletal system supports movement and also produces blood cells in the bone marrow.

questions.question: Which of the following is a source of protein?
questions.explanation: Pulses are rich in protein, while rice gives carbohydrates, apple gives vitamins and butter gives fats.

questions.question: Statement A: Sound travels faster in air than in water. Statement B: Sound cannot travel through vacuum. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and B are correct
questions.explanation: Sound travels faster in water than in air, so A is false. Sound cannot travel through vacuum, so B is true.

questions.question: A moving object always stops on its own.
questions.explanation: An object continues to move unless an external force acts on it.

questions.question: Which of the following are sources of vitamin C? (i) Lemon, (ii) Orange, (iii) Milk, (iv) Amla
questions.explanation: Vitamin C is found in citrus fruits like lemon and orange, and also in amla.

questions.question: The phenomenon of light bouncing back from a surface is called:
questions.explanation: Reflection is the bouncing back of light when it strikes a surface.

questions.question: Sound can travel through vacuum.
questions.explanation: Sound needs a medium to travel and cannot travel through vacuum.

questions.question: The device used to measure temperature is:
questions.explanation: A thermometer is used to measure temperature.

questions.question: Which material is strongly attracted to a magnet?
questions.explanation: Iron and its alloys are strongly attracted to magnets.

questions.question: Biodegradable wastes can be broken down by microorganisms.
questions.explanation: Biodegradable wastes are decomposed by microbes naturally.

questions.question: Which of the following is a medicinal plant?
questions.explanation: Neem is widely used in traditional medicine for its healing properties.

questions.question: A force that opposes the motion of an object is called:
questions.explanation: Frictional force always opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact.

questions.question: Which of the following is an invertebrate?
questions.explanation: Earthworm does not have a backbone, so it is an invertebrate. Fish, frog and snake are vertebrates.

questions.question: Which vitamin is known as ascorbic acid?
questions.explanation: Ascorbic acid is the chemical name for Vitamin C, which is essential for immunity.

questions.question: The image formed by a plane mirror is:
questions.explanation: A plane mirror always forms a virtual and erect image of the same size as the object.

questions.question: Sound cannot travel through:
questions.explanation: Sound requires a medium to travel; it cannot travel through a vacuum because there are no particles to vibrate.

questions.question: A closed path through which electric current flows is called:
questions.explanation: An electric circuit is a closed conducting path that allows current to flow from the cell to the bulb.

questions.question: The ends of a magnet are called:
questions.explanation: Every magnet has two ends called poles – north pole and south pole – where magnetic force is strongest.

questions.question: All metals are magnetic.
questions.explanation: Only some metals like iron, nickel and cobalt are magnetic; most metals like copper, gold and aluminium are not.

questions.question: True or False: All magnets have two poles – North and South.
questions.explanation: Every magnet, regardless of shape, has a North pole and a South pole.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is responsible for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll and are the main sites for photosynthesis.

questions.question: True or False: Plants are called producers in a food chain.
questions.explanation: Plants produce their own food through photosynthesis, so they are the producers in a food chain.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct? (i) The SI unit of mass is kilogram. (ii) The SI unit of time is minute. (iii) The SI unit of temperature is Kelvin. (iv) The SI unit of electric current is ampere.
questions.explanation: The SI unit of time is second, not minute. So statements (i), (iii), and (iv) are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a push force?
questions.explanation: Kicking a ball is a push force, while the others are examples of pull or squeeze forces. (Term I – Force and Motion)

questions.question: The SI unit of length is the metre.
questions.explanation: The SI unit of length is the metre. (Term I – Measurements)

questions.question: Which of the following is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Water is a liquid at room temperature. Oxygen is a gas, iron and stone are solids. (Term I – Matter around Us)

questions.question: Statement (i): Matter is anything that has mass and occupies space. Statement (ii): Non-living things can grow. Which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: Matter has mass and occupies space. Non-living things do not grow. (Term I – Matter around Us)

questions.question: Which part of the human body is responsible for pumping blood?
questions.explanation: The heart pumps blood throughout the body. (Term I – Health and Hygiene)

questions.question: All animals are unicellular.
questions.explanation: Many animals are multicellular. Only some organisms like amoeba are unicellular. (Term I – The World of Animals)

questions.question: Which part of the plant is known as the food factory?
questions.explanation: Leaves produce food through photosynthesis. (Term I – The World of Plants)

questions.question: Water is made up of hydrogen and oxygen.
questions.explanation: Water molecule (H₂O) contains two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom. (Term III – Water)

questions.question: Which of the following is a modified stem?
questions.explanation: Potato is a modified stem (tuber) that stores food, while carrot, radish, and turnip are modified roots.

questions.question: The image formed by a plane mirror is always virtual and erect.
questions.explanation: A plane mirror always produces a virtual, erect image of the same size as the object.

questions.question: Deficiency of vitamin C causes which disease?
questions.explanation: Vitamin C deficiency leads to scurvy, characterized by bleeding gums and weakness.

questions.question: All animals are heterotrophs.
questions.explanation: Animals cannot make their own food and depend on other organisms for nutrition, so they are heterotrophs.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and allows electric current to flow through it easily, making it a good conductor.

questions.question: Which of the following is a biotic component of an ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Biotic components are living things; plants are living, while sunlight, soil, and water are abiotic.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct? (A) All metals are solids at room temperature. (B) Non-metals are always gases. (C) Metals are good conductors of heat and electricity. (D) Non-metals are malleable.
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors of heat and electricity; not all metals are solids (e.g., mercury is liquid), and non-metals can be solids too, from Matter around Us.

questions.question: What is the main function of roots in plants?
questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil, as taught in The World of Plants.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of simple machine?
questions.explanation: A battery is not a simple machine; lever, pulley, and inclined plane are, from Force and Motion.

questions.question: The SI unit of mass is kilogram.
questions.explanation: The kilogram is the SI base unit of mass, as per the Measurements unit.

questions.question: Which animal group is characterized by feathers?
questions.explanation: Birds are the only animals with feathers, as covered in 'The World of Animals'.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.explanation: The newton is the SI unit of force, introduced in the Force and Motion unit.

questions.question: Matter can be classified into solids, liquids, and gases.
questions.explanation: This classification is the basis of the 'Matter around Us' unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and conducts electricity; others are insulators.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-contact force?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force acts without physical contact, as explained in the Force and Motion unit of the Samacheer textbook.

questions.question: True or False: Sound can travel through vacuum.
questions.explanation: Sound requires a medium to travel; it cannot travel through vacuum, as taught in the Sound chapter of the Samacheer curriculum.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is responsible for reproduction in flowering plants?
questions.explanation: The flower is the reproductive part of a plant, as explained in The World of Plants unit.

questions.question: Statement A: All living things need food. Statement B: All living things can move. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: All living things need food for energy, but not all can move (e.g., plants), as per The World of Animals unit.

questions.question: What is the main function of the skeleton system?
questions.explanation: The skeleton provides support and protects internal organs, as taught in Health and Hygiene unit.

questions.question: True or False: The moon is a natural source of light.
questions.explanation: The moon reflects sunlight; it is not a natural source of light, as per the Light concepts in the textbook.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a state of matter?
questions.explanation: Energy is not a state of matter; the three states are solid, liquid, and gas, as per Matter around Us unit.

questions.question: Statement A: Sound can travel through vacuum. Statement B: Light can travel through vacuum. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: Sound requires a medium to travel, but light can travel through vacuum, as taught in the Force and Motion and related units.

questions.question: Which of the following is a method of separation used to separate grains from stalks?
questions.explanation: Threshing is the process of beating grains to separate them from stalks, as per the Matter around Us unit.

questions.question: True or False: All magnets have two poles: North and South.
questions.explanation: Every magnet has a north and south pole, a fundamental concept from the Magnetism unit in Term 3.

questions.question: Which of the following is a water-soluble vitamin?
questions.explanation: Vitamin C is water-soluble, while vitamins A, D, and K are fat-soluble, as per the Health and Hygiene unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of periodic motion?
options: A car moving on a straight road
A leaf falling from a tree
questions.explanation: Periodic motion repeats at regular intervals. A swinging pendulum is a classic example of periodic motion.

questions.question: Which part of a plant helps in the absorption of water and minerals from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil and transport them to other parts of the plant.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by a virus?
questions.explanation: The common cold is caused by a virus, while the others are caused by bacteria.

questions.question: Statement: A freely suspended magnet always points in the north-south direction. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: The statement is true because Earth itself behaves like a giant magnet.
The statement is false because a magnet points in any direction.
The statement is true only for bar magnets.
The statement is true only when the magnet is placed in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: A freely suspended magnet aligns itself along the Earth's magnetic field, pointing north-south.

questions.question: Statement: Water can dissolve many substances, so it is called a universal solvent. Which of the following is a correct explanation?
options: Water can dissolve more substances than any other liquid.
questions.explanation: Water is called a universal solvent because it dissolves a wide variety of substances, more than any other liquid.

questions.question: True or False: All insects have six legs. Convert this statement into a correct MCQ:
options: True, because insects belong to the class Insecta.
False, because some insects like spiders have eight legs.
True, because all arthropods have six legs.
False, because only flies have six legs.
questions.explanation: Insects are characterized by having three pairs of legs (six legs). Spiders are arachnids, not insects.

questions.question: Which of the following are components of our environment? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Our environment consists of land, air, and water along with living organisms.

questions.question: A ball rolling on the ground gradually slows down and stops. This is due to the force of:
questions.explanation: Friction opposes motion and causes moving objects to slow down and stop.

questions.question: The method used to separate sand and water is:
questions.explanation: Filtration is used to separate insoluble solids like sand from liquids.

questions.question: Which nutrient is mainly responsible for building and repairing body tissues?
questions.explanation: Proteins are essential for growth and repair of body tissues.

questions.question: A force can change the shape of an object.
questions.explanation: Force can change the shape, size, or motion of an object. This is covered in Force and Motion.

questions.question: Water is known as a universal solvent.
questions.explanation: Water dissolves many substances, hence it is called the universal solvent.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non‑renewable natural resource?
questions.explanation: Coal takes millions of years to form and is non‑renewable, while sunlight, wind and water are renewable.

questions.question: The CPU is considered the brain of a computer.
questions.explanation: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) performs all calculations and controls other parts, acting as the brain.

questions.question: What is the unit of force in the SI system?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of force is the newton (N), as studied in Force and Motion.

questions.question: What is the force that opposes the motion of an object called?
questions.explanation: Friction is the force that opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact.

questions.question: Which of the following animals is an invertebrate?
questions.explanation: A snail is an invertebrate as it lacks a backbone, unlike fish, frogs, and snakes which are vertebrates.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: The moon is a natural satellite of Earth.
questions.explanation: The moon is a natural satellite that orbits Earth; the sun is a star, and Earth revolves around the sun.

questions.question: Which of the following is a physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting of ice is a physical change because it changes state but no new substance is formed.

questions.question: Matter has mass and occupies space.
questions.explanation: Matter is anything that has mass and occupies volume.

questions.question: Which vitamin deficiency causes night blindness?
questions.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency leads to night blindness and other vision problems.

questions.question: The angle of incidence is always equal to the angle of reflection.
questions.explanation: This is the first law of reflection: the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection.

questions.question: Which of the following is a renewable source of water?
questions.explanation: Rainwater is continuously renewed through the water cycle, making it a renewable source.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about food chain is correct?
options: A food chain always starts with a herbivore.
A food chain shows the flow of energy from one organism to another.
A food chain always ends with a producer.
A food chain consists of only plants.
questions.explanation: A food chain illustrates the transfer of energy through different trophic levels, starting with producers and ending with top consumers.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an element?
questions.explanation: Water is a compound made of hydrogen and oxygen, while oxygen, iron, and gold are elements.

questions.question: Which is the SI unit of length?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of length is metre, as defined in the International System of Units.

questions.question: Which force always opposes motion between two surfaces in contact?
questions.explanation: Frictional force acts opposite to the direction of motion, opposing relative motion between surfaces.

questions.question: Which state of matter has a fixed shape and fixed volume?
questions.explanation: Solids have a definite shape and volume because their particles are closely packed in a fixed arrangement.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is primarily responsible for preparing food through photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll and are the main sites for photosynthesis, converting light energy into chemical energy.

questions.question: Which of the following animals has a backbone and is classified as a vertebrate?
questions.explanation: Fish possess a vertebral column (backbone), placing them in the vertebrate group.

questions.question: True or False: Plants that grow in extremely dry areas are called xerophytes.
questions.explanation: Xerophytes are adapted to survive with minimal water in arid regions.

questions.question: Which of the following are sources of water on Earth? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Rivers, lakes, and oceans are major water sources; a desert is a dry area lacking surface water.

questions.question: Which gas is commonly used in fire extinguishers because it does not support combustion?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide displaces oxygen and smothers fire, making it effective in extinguishers.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced in the human body when the skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Sunlight triggers synthesis of vitamin D in the skin, essential for bone health.

questions.question: True or False: The SI unit of force is the newton.
questions.explanation: The newton is the SI unit for force, defined as the force needed to accelerate one kilogram at one metre per second squared.

questions.question: The SI unit of length is
questions.explanation: The SI unit of length is metre (m). Centimetre, kilometre and millimetre are other commonly used units.

questions.question: In which state of matter do particles have the maximum freedom of movement?
questions.explanation: In gases, particles are far apart and move freely in all directions, giving them maximum freedom.

questions.question: True or False: All animals with a backbone are called vertebrates.
questions.explanation: Vertebrates are animals that have a backbone (vertebral column). Examples include fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct? (i) A food chain always starts with a producer. (ii) Herbivores are also called primary consumers. (iii) Carnivores eat only plants. (iv) Decomposers break down dead organisms.
questions.explanation: Statement (iii) is false because carnivores eat other animals, not plants. The correct statements are (i), (ii) and (iv).

questions.question: Which plant part is commonly used as a source of fibre for making cloth?
questions.explanation: Stems of plants like jute and flax provide fibres used for making cloth. Cotton fibres come from the fruit (boll).

questions.question: An object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force. This is Newton's first law of motion.
questions.explanation: This statement correctly describes Newton's first law of motion, also known as the law of inertia.

questions.question: Which pigment is responsible for photosynthesis in plants?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in plants that captures light energy for photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is attracted to a magnet?
questions.explanation: Iron is a ferromagnetic material and is strongly attracted to magnets.

questions.question: Water is a universal solvent.
questions.explanation: Water is called the universal solvent because it dissolves more substances than any other liquid.

questions.question: The process of converting a liquid into a vapour at a temperature below its boiling point is called?
questions.explanation: Evaporation occurs when a liquid turns into vapour at any temperature below its boiling point.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is a source of turmeric?
questions.explanation: Turmeric is obtained from the rhizome, which is an underground stem of the Curcuma longa plant.

questions.question: All animals are multicellular and heterotrophic.
questions.explanation: Animals are multicellular organisms that obtain nutrients by consuming other organisms (heterotrophic).

questions.question: All matter is made up of tiny particles called atoms.
questions.explanation: Matter is composed of atoms and molecules.

questions.question: Which of the following is a natural indicator?
questions.explanation: Litmus, turmeric and china rose are all natural indicators.

questions.question: Which of the following are biodegradable? (i) paper (ii) plastic (iii) cotton (iv) glass
questions.explanation: Paper and cotton are biodegradable; plastic and glass are non-biodegradable.

questions.question: Which plant is used to make coir?
questions.explanation: Coir is made from the husk of coconut.

questions.question: Which device is used to input data into a computer?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is an input device used to type data into a computer.

questions.question: Photosynthesis mainly takes place in which part of the plant?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll and are the primary site for photosynthesis in plants.

questions.question: Carbohydrates are body-building foods.
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates provide energy; proteins are the body-building foods.

questions.question: Which of the following is a natural magnet?
questions.explanation: Lodestone (magnetite) is a naturally occurring magnetic rock, while others are artificial magnets.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a step in the water cycle?
questions.explanation: Filtration is not part of the natural water cycle; it is a man-made purification process.

questions.question: Turmeric turns red in acidic solutions.
questions.explanation: Turmeric is a natural indicator that turns red in basic (alkaline) solutions, not acidic.

questions.question: Which of the following is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Metals like iron are good conductors of heat, while wood, plastic, and air are poor conductors (insulators).

questions.question: Solids have definite shape and volume.
questions.explanation: Solids have a fixed shape and volume because their particles are tightly packed.

questions.question: Red litmus paper turns blue in a basic solution.
questions.explanation: Red litmus turns blue in bases, while blue litmus turns red in acids.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of motion where an object moves around a fixed point?
questions.explanation: Circular motion is the movement of an object along a circular path around a fixed centre, as taught in Force and Motion.

questions.question: Which part of a plant is responsible for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis to prepare food, as covered in The World of Plants.

questions.question: Which vitamin is known as ascorbic acid and helps prevent scurvy?
questions.explanation: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) prevents scurvy, as taught in Health and Hygiene.

questions.question: True or False: A mouse is an output device of a computer.
questions.explanation: A mouse is an input device, not an output device, as per the Computer - An Introduction unit.

questions.question: Which property of light causes the formation of shadows?
questions.explanation: Light travels in straight lines (rectilinear propagation), leading to shadow formation, as per the Light unit.

questions.question: Which of these materials is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Metals like iron are good conductors, while rubber, wood, and plastic are insulators, per the Electricity unit.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about water is correct?
options: Water is a compound of hydrogen and oxygen.
Water boils at 100°C at standard pressure.
Ice is denser than liquid water.
Water is used as a universal solvent.
questions.explanation: Water is composed of hydrogen and oxygen (H2O). Ice is less dense than water, while other statements are also true but the question asks for a correct statement; typically the compound nature is fundamental.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about force: (i) Force can change the direction of a moving object. (ii) Force can change the shape of an object. (iii) Force can increase the speed of an object. (iv) Force can change the mass of an object. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Force can change direction, shape, and speed of an object, but it cannot change its mass. Mass is constant. This is from the Force and Motion unit of Term 1.

questions.question: Magnet attracts all types of metals.
questions.explanation: Magnets attract only ferromagnetic materials like iron, nickel, and cobalt, not all metals. This concept is from the Magnetism unit of Term 3.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of gases?
options: Definite volume but no definite shape
Definite shape but no definite volume
No definite shape and no definite volume
questions.explanation: Gases do not have a fixed shape or volume. They take the shape and volume of their container, unlike solids and liquids.

questions.question: Which of the following is the SI unit of mass?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of mass is kilogram (kg), as defined in the chapter on Measurements (Class 6, Term 1).

questions.question: Ravi observed that a ball rolling on the ground comes to rest after some time. Which force is primarily responsible for this?
questions.explanation: Friction opposes the motion between surfaces in contact. The ball slows down due to friction between the ball and the ground, as per the chapter on Force and Motion (Class 6, Term 1).

questions.question: In the chapter 'Matter around Us', which of the following is classified as a pure substance?
questions.explanation: Gold is made of only one type of atom, making it a pure substance. Mixtures like salt solution, milk, and air contain more than one component (Class 6, Term 1).

questions.question: Statement A: Spiders have more than four pairs of legs. Statement B: Insects like cockroaches have three pairs of legs. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and B are correct
Both A and B are wrong
questions.explanation: Spiders belong to Arachnids and have four pairs of legs. Insects have three pairs of legs. Both statements are correct (Class 6, Term 1 - The World of Animals).

questions.question: True or False: Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
questions.explanation: Sound requires a medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to travel. It cannot travel through a vacuum (Class 6, Term 2 - Sound, though inherited from general science principles).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of motion?
questions.explanation: Chemical motion is not a standard type of motion in physics. Random, periodic, and linear are valid types (Class 6, Term 2 - Force and Motion).

questions.question: True or False: Friction always works against the direction of motion.
questions.explanation: Frictional force opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact, so it acts opposite to the direction of motion (Class 6, Term 2 - Force and Motion).

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the liver?
questions.explanation: The liver produces bile juice, which helps in the digestion of fats. This is a key point from the chapter on Nutrition (Class 6, Term 3).

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct SI unit of length?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of length is the metre, as defined in the Measurements unit of the Samacheer Kalvi Class 6 Science textbook.

questions.question: Which of the following animals is classified as an invertebrate?
questions.explanation: Earthworms are invertebrates as they lack a backbone, while fish, frogs, and snakes are vertebrates, as per the World of Animals unit.

questions.question: True or False: The human body has 206 bones at birth.
questions.explanation: False. A newborn baby has about 300 bones, which fuse to form 206 bones in adulthood, as per the Health and Hygiene unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary source of vitamin C?
questions.explanation: Citrus fruits like oranges and lemons are rich in vitamin C, as covered in the Health and Hygiene unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a solid?
questions.explanation: Solids have a fixed shape and volume, unlike liquids and gases, as per the Matter around Us unit.

questions.question: True or False: The SI unit of mass is the kilogram.
questions.explanation: True. The kilogram is the SI unit of mass, as defined in the Measurements unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of simple machine?
questions.explanation: A lever is a simple machine, while battery, motor, and bulb are not, as per the Force and Motion unit.

questions.question: In the chapter 'Force and Motion', what is the force that opposes the motion of an object when it is in contact with a surface?
questions.explanation: Frictional force is the force that opposes the motion of an object when it is in contact with another surface. This is a key concept in the Force and Motion unit.

questions.question: The process by which plants make their own food is called photosynthesis. Which of the following is NOT a necessary component for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is a by-product of photosynthesis, not a requirement. The necessary components are sunlight, chlorophyll, carbon dioxide, and water.

questions.question: Statement: 'All matter is made up of tiny particles called atoms.' Is this statement true or false?
questions.explanation: The statement is true. The textbook explains that matter is composed of atoms, which are the basic building blocks.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a solid state of matter?
options: It has a definite shape but no definite volume.
It has no definite shape or volume.
It has a definite shape and a definite volume.
It has a definite volume but no definite shape.
questions.explanation: Solids have a definite shape and a definite volume. This is a fundamental property of solids as described in the chapter 'Matter around Us'.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a method of separation of mixtures?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a biological process, not a method for separating mixtures. Evaporation, distillation, and filtration are all physical separation techniques.

questions.question: In the chapter 'The World of Plants', which part of the plant is responsible for the transport of water from the roots to the leaves?
questions.explanation: Xylem is the vascular tissue that transports water and minerals from the roots to the leaves. Phloem transports food.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a periodic motion?
options: A car moving in a straight line
A leaf falling from a tree
questions.explanation: A pendulum swinging repeats its motion at regular intervals, which is periodic motion. The other options are not repetitive in a fixed time interval.

questions.question: In the context of force and motion, what is the effect of an unbalanced force on a moving object?
options: It keeps the object moving at constant speed
It changes the speed or direction of the object
It has no effect on the object
questions.explanation: An unbalanced force changes the state of motion, altering speed or direction, as per Term I Force and Motion.

questions.question: Which of the following is used as a preservative in pickles?
questions.explanation: Salt, sugar, and vinegar are all used as preservatives in food, as per Chemistry in Everyday Life.

questions.question: True or False: Plants in daily life provide us with medicines, fibres, and timber.
questions.explanation: Plants are sources of medicines, fibres, timber, and many other products, as per Plants in Daily Life.

questions.question: In the context of the periodic table, which of the following statements about elements is correct?
options: All elements are made up of atoms.
Elements can be broken down into simpler substances by chemical reactions.
questions.explanation: Elements are pure substances made of only one type of atom. They cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means. Hydrogen is the lightest element, and gold is a metal.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a shadow?
options: It shows the details of the object.
It is formed when light is blocked by an opaque object.
It can be formed on a transparent screen.
questions.explanation: A shadow is formed when an opaque object blocks the path of light. It is always black, does not show details, and requires a screen.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the stem in a plant?
options: Conducting water and minerals to the leaves
questions.explanation: The stem conducts water and minerals from the roots to the leaves and also supports the plant.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of gases?
options: They have a fixed melting point
questions.explanation: Gases do not have a fixed melting point; they change directly to liquid or solid under specific conditions. The other three are correct properties of gases.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is primarily responsible for transpiration?
questions.explanation: Leaves have stomata through which water vapour is lost to the atmosphere – this process is called transpiration.

questions.question: Which of the following animals is NOT a vertebrate?
questions.explanation: Earthworm is an invertebrate (no backbone). Frog, snake and fish are vertebrates with a backbone.

questions.question: Regular reflection of light occurs from a rough surface.
questions.explanation: Regular reflection occurs from a smooth, polished surface. Rough surfaces cause diffuse reflection.

questions.question: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration, providing energy for the cell, hence called powerhouse.

questions.question: In a food chain, which of the following is a primary consumer?
questions.explanation: Primary consumers are herbivores that eat producers (plants). Rabbit feeds on grass, so it is a primary consumer.

questions.question: Which plant is commonly used to treat cough and cold?
questions.explanation: Tulsi (holy basil) is widely used in traditional medicine for cough, cold and respiratory ailments.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Measurement: I. The SI unit of length is centimetre. II. A stopwatch is used to measure time interval accurately. III. The volume of a liquid is measured in kilogram. Which of the following combinations is correct?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of length is metre, not centimetre. Kilogram is the unit of mass, not volume. A stopwatch measures time intervals accurately.

questions.question: When a coin is placed on a cardboard sheet over a glass and the cardboard is flicked sharply, the coin drops into the glass. This observation demonstrates which property?
options: The coin is acted upon by gravitational force only
The coin possesses inertia of rest
The cardboard applies a forward force on the coin
The coin loses its mass momentarily
questions.explanation: The coin tends to remain in its state of rest due to inertia while the cardboard moves away, causing it to fall into the glass.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about changes of state is TRUE? I. The melting point of ice is 100°C at atmospheric pressure. II. Evaporation is a surface phenomenon that occurs at all temperatures. III. Condensation is the change of state from gas to liquid. IV. Sublimation is the direct change from solid to gas.
questions.explanation: The melting point of ice is 0°C, not 100°C. Statements II, III and IV are correct definitions from the lesson Matter around Us.

questions.question: Which of the following is an INCORRECT match regarding animal adaptations?
options: Camel: Hump stores fat for desert survival
Duck: Webbed feet assist in swimming
Fish: Lungs help in gas exchange under water
questions.explanation: Fish use gills for gas exchange underwater, not lungs. Lungs are respiratory organs in terrestrial animals.

questions.question: In a closed room, air near the ceiling is usually warmer than air near the floor. This happens because heat transfer in air occurs mainly by:
questions.explanation: Warm air expands, becomes less dense and rises; cool air sinks, creating convection currents that transfer heat throughout the room.

questions.question: In an activity, three bulbs are connected to a cell. When one bulb is removed, the other two stop glowing. This observation shows that the bulbs are connected in:
questions.explanation: In a series connection, components are linked one after the other. If one component breaks, the circuit becomes open and current stops flowing through all components.

questions.question: Bile juice stored in the gall bladder helps in the digestion of proteins.
questions.explanation: Bile juice emulsifies fats; it does not digest proteins. Protein digestion is carried out by enzymes like pepsin and trypsin.

questions.question: A bar magnet is cut into two equal pieces vertically along its length. Which of the following is true about each piece?
options: Each piece becomes a weaker magnet with no poles.
Each piece becomes an independent magnet with both North and South poles.
The cut faces lose their magnetic properties completely.
Each piece becomes a single-pole magnet.
questions.explanation: A magnet always has two poles regardless of its size. When cut, each piece forms a new independent magnet with both North and South poles.

questions.question: Read the following statements about Chemistry in Everyday Life and choose the correct combination. I. Baking soda is used in bakery products and as an antacid. II. Common salt is chemically known as sodium chloride. III. Soft soap is prepared using sodium hydroxide and oil. IV. Bleaching powder is used to disinfect drinking water.
questions.explanation: Soft soap is made using potassium hydroxide, whereas sodium hydroxide produces hard soap. Statements I, II and IV are correct.

questions.question: In the food chain ‘Grass → Grasshopper → Frog → Snake → Eagle’, which organism occupies the third trophic level?
questions.explanation: Grass is the producer (1st trophic level), Grasshopper is the primary consumer (2nd), Frog is the secondary consumer (3rd), Snake is the tertiary consumer (4th), and Eagle is the quaternary consumer (5th).

questions.question: In the context of the periodic table, which of the following statements is true about elements?
options: A) Elements are made up of two or more types of atoms.
B) Elements are pure substances that cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means.
C) Elements are mixtures of compounds.
D) Elements are always found in liquid state at room temperature.
questions.explanation: Elements are pure substances consisting of only one type of atom, and they cannot be broken down into simpler substances by ordinary chemical reactions.

questions.question: Which of the following animals belongs to the group of invertebrates?
questions.explanation: Earthworms are invertebrates as they lack a backbone, while fish, frogs, and snakes are vertebrates, as per The World of Animals unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a method to purify water at home?
questions.explanation: Decantation, filtration, and boiling are all methods to purify water, as covered in the Water unit of Term II.

questions.question: Which of the following is a biodegradable waste?
questions.explanation: Vegetable peels are biodegradable as they can be decomposed by microorganisms, while plastic, glass, and metal are non-biodegradable, as per Our Environment unit.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is commonly used as a spice in daily life?
questions.explanation: Cumin seeds are used as a spice, while carrot root, potato stem, and spinach leaves are vegetables, as per Plants in Daily Life unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a living organism?
questions.explanation: Growth, reproduction, and response to stimuli are all characteristics of living organisms, as per The World of Animals and The World of Plants units.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a magnet?
options: It has two poles: North and South
It can be used to generate electricity always
It loses its property when broken
questions.explanation: A magnet always has two poles, North and South, as explained in the Magnetism unit of Term III.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the environment is correct?
options: The environment includes only living things
The environment includes only non-living things
The environment includes both living and non-living things
The environment is only the air around us
questions.explanation: The environment consists of both biotic (living) and abiotic (non-living) components, as defined in the Our Environment unit.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is commonly used as a spice from the stem?
questions.explanation: Cinnamon is obtained from the bark (stem) of the cinnamon tree, as per the Plants in Daily Life unit.

questions.question: True or False: All metals are magnetic.
questions.explanation: Only certain metals like iron, nickel, and cobalt are magnetic; most metals are not, as per the Magnetism unit.

questions.question: True or False: A magnet can attract all metals.
questions.explanation: Magnets attract only ferromagnetic materials like iron, nickel, and cobalt, not all metals, as per the Magnetism unit in Term III.

questions.question: Which of the following is a method to purify water?
questions.explanation: Boiling kills germs and is a common method to purify water, as per the Water unit in Term II.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is used to make turmeric powder?
questions.explanation: Turmeric is obtained from the stem (rhizome) of the turmeric plant, as per the Plants in Daily Life unit.

questions.question: Statement A: Sound can travel through a vacuum. Statement B: Light can travel through a vacuum. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and B are true
A is true, B is false
A is false, B is true
Both A and B are false
questions.explanation: Sound cannot travel through a vacuum as it needs a medium, but light can travel through a vacuum, as per the Sound and Light concepts in the curriculum.

questions.question: True or False: The human heart is located in the abdominal cavity.
questions.explanation: The human heart is located in the thoracic cavity, not the abdominal cavity, as per the Health and Hygiene unit.

questions.question: In the context of water, what is the term for the process of water changing from liquid to vapour?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is the process of water turning into vapour, as covered in the Water unit of Term III.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Rusting of iron is a chemical change because it forms a new substance, as per the Chemistry in Everyday Life unit.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct relation between meter and centimeter?
questions.explanation: The standard unit of length is meter. 1 meter is equal to 100 centimeters.

questions.question: Observe the following statements about matter: (i) Solids have definite shape and volume. (ii) Liquids have definite volume but no definite shape. (iii) Gases have neither definite shape nor definite volume. (iv) The particles in a solid are tightly packed. Which statement is incorrect?
questions.explanation: All the given statements correctly describe the properties of solids, liquids, and gases according to the chapter 'Matter around Us'.

questions.question: State whether the following is true or false: 'Roots of a plant absorb water and minerals from the soil.'
questions.explanation: Roots are the part of the plant that anchor it and absorb water and dissolved minerals from the soil.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Metals like copper are good conductors of heat. Wood, plastic, and air are insulators.

questions.question: In an electric circuit, the component that provides the path for current to flow is called a
questions.explanation: Wires connect the components and provide a path for the electric current to flow. The cell provides energy, the switch controls flow, and the bulb converts electrical energy to light.

questions.question: State true or false: 'Deforestation leads to an increase in biodiversity.'
questions.explanation: Deforestation destroys habitats and reduces biodiversity, not increases it. The statement is false.

questions.question: Which instrument is used to measure the length of a curved line?
questions.explanation: To measure curved lines, a thread is used along the curve and then measured on a scale.

questions.question: The SI unit of mass is kilogram (True/False).
questions.explanation: The SI unit of mass is kilogram, as defined in the measurement chapter.

questions.question: Which pair shows a correct example of contact force?
questions.explanation: Friction is a contact force acting between surfaces in contact.

questions.question: The 'periodic table' of elements arranges elements in order of increasing atomic number. Which of the following is a noble gas?
questions.explanation: Helium is a noble gas in Group 18 of the periodic table.

questions.question: True or False: Air, water, and soil are examples of natural resources.
questions.explanation: Natural resources are obtained from nature; air, water, soil are natural.

questions.question: Which part of the plant anchors it to the soil and absorbs water?
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant and absorb water and minerals from the soil.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the human digestive system?
questions.explanation: Lungs are part of the respiratory system, not digestive.

questions.question: Which disease is caused by deficiency of Vitamin C?
questions.explanation: Scurvy is caused by deficiency of Vitamin C.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about force is correct?
options: Force can only be a push or a pull applied by living beings.
Friction is an example of a non-contact force.
Magnetic force is a contact force.
Force can change the shape of an object.
questions.explanation: Force can change the shape, size, or motion of an object. Friction is a contact force, magnetic force is a non-contact force, and force can be applied by non-living things as well.

questions.question: Which of the following are properties of solids? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Solids have definite shape and definite volume. They are not highly compressible and generally have higher density than gases.

questions.question: Which of the following animals has a three-chambered heart?
questions.explanation: Frogs (amphibians) have a three-chambered heart with two atria and one ventricle. Fish have two chambers, crocodiles and birds have four chambers.

questions.question: The spice obtained from the bark of a tree is ______.
questions.explanation: Cinnamon is obtained from the inner bark of trees of the genus Cinnamomum. Pepper comes from berries, clove from flower buds, cardamom from seeds.

questions.question: A body moves in a circular path with constant speed. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: The direction of motion changes continuously.
The force acting on the body is zero.
questions.explanation: In circular motion, direction changes continuously, so velocity changes and acceleration exists. Force is required (centripetal). Only statement 2 is correct.

questions.question: True or False: All animals that have a backbone are called invertebrates.
questions.explanation: Animals with a backbone are called vertebrates. Invertebrates lack a backbone.

questions.question: In a bar magnet, the magnetic field is strongest at the:
questions.explanation: The magnetic field is strongest at the poles (both north and south) of a bar magnet.

questions.question: Which of the following processes is not part of the water cycle?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a biological process in plants, not a step in the water cycle. Evaporation, condensation, and precipitation are key processes.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about acids and bases: (i) Acids turn blue litmus red. (ii) Bases turn red litmus blue. (iii) Acids have a bitter taste. (iv) Bases are sour in taste. Which statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus red and are sour; bases turn red litmus blue and are bitter. So statements (i) and (ii) are correct; (iii) and (iv) are swapped.

questions.question: In a food chain, which organism is a primary consumer?
questions.explanation: Primary consumers are herbivores that eat plants. Rabbit eats grass; grass is a producer, snake and eagle are secondary/tertiary consumers.

questions.question: The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection. This law is applicable to:
questions.explanation: The law of reflection (angle i = angle r) holds for all types of mirrors, including plane, concave, and convex.

questions.question: True or False: Metals are good conductors of heat, while wood is a good conductor.
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors of heat, but wood is a poor conductor (insulator). So the statement is false.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about force is correct? (i) Force can change the shape of an object. (ii) Force can change the direction of a moving object. (iii) Force can be measured in kilograms. (iv) Force can stop a moving object.
questions.explanation: Force can change shape, direction, and stop motion. Force is measured in newton, not kilogram. So (iii) is false.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false: 'All animals have a backbone and are called vertebrates.'
questions.explanation: False. Animals without backbone are invertebrates (e.g., insects, worms). Only some animals are vertebrates.

questions.question: Which of the following is a communicable disease caused by a virus?
questions.explanation: Chickenpox is caused by varicella virus and spreads from person to person. Others are non-communicable.

questions.question: In which mode of heat transfer does the heat travel without any medium?
questions.explanation: Radiation does not require a medium; it travels through vacuum (e.g., heat from the Sun).

questions.question: Which of the following statements about animals is/are correct? (i) Fish have scales and fins. (ii) Amphibians can live both on land and in water. (iii) Birds have hollow bones. (iv) Mammals lay eggs.
questions.explanation: Fish have scales and fins; amphibians live on land and water; birds have hollow bones for flight. Mammals give birth to young ones, not lay eggs (except platypus, but not typical). So (iv) is false.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false: 'The north pole of a magnet attracts the north pole of another magnet.'
questions.explanation: False. Like poles repel; north pole repels north pole. Opposite poles attract.

questions.question: Which of the following is a compound?
questions.explanation: Water (H₂O) is a compound made of hydrogen and oxygen. Oxygen, iron, and carbon are elements.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced by the human body when exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin upon exposure to sunlight. It helps in calcium absorption.

questions.question: Which of the following is used as an antacid?
questions.explanation: Baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) is a mild base used to neutralize excess stomach acid, acting as an antacid.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is not attracted to a magnet?
questions.explanation: Copper is non-magnetic and is not attracted to a magnet. Iron, nickel, and cobalt are ferromagnetic materials.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct? (i) The moon is a natural satellite. (ii) The earth is a planet. (iii) The sun is a star. (iv) All of the above.
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct: the moon orbits Earth as a natural satellite, Earth is a planet, and the Sun is a star.

questions.question: Which of the following animals belongs to the group 'Arthropoda'?
questions.explanation: Butterfly has jointed legs and an exoskeleton, characteristic of Arthropoda. Earthworm is Annelida, starfish is Echinodermata, snail is Mollusca.

questions.question: A freely suspended bar magnet always comes to rest in the North-South direction. (True/False)
questions.explanation: A freely suspended magnet aligns itself with the Earth's magnetic field, pointing roughly North-South.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about common salt (sodium chloride) are correct? (i) It is obtained from seawater by evaporation. (ii) It is used to preserve food. (iii) It is a compound of sodium and chlorine. (iv) It is a mixture of two elements.
questions.explanation: Salt is a compound, not a mixture, so statement (iv) is false. Statements (i), (ii), (iii) are correct.

questions.question: Which disease is caused by a deficiency of Vitamin C?
questions.explanation: Scurvy is caused by lack of Vitamin C. Rickets is Vitamin D, Beriberi is Vitamin B1, Anaemia is iron deficiency.

questions.question: Which of the following impurities in water can be removed by boiling?
questions.explanation: Boiling kills germs (microorganisms). Sand can be filtered, dissolved salts remain, oil floats and can be skimmed.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about cells is correct?
options: Plant cells have a cell wall.
questions.explanation: Plant cells have a cell wall made of cellulose, while animal cells do not. This is from Term II Cell Structure.

questions.question: Which of the following are natural fibres? (i) Cotton (ii) Nylon (iii) Jute (iv) Polyester
questions.explanation: Cotton and jute are natural plant fibres; nylon and polyester are synthetic. This is from Term III Chemistry in Everyday Life.

questions.question: Which organ is responsible for pumping blood in the human body?
questions.explanation: The heart pumps blood throughout the body. This is from Term II Human Organ Systems.

questions.question: Which of the following animals has a backbone?
questions.explanation: Fish are vertebrates with a backbone; earthworms, snails, and starfish are invertebrates. From Term I The World of Animals.

questions.question: Which plant is used to make the medicine for malaria?
questions.explanation: Cinchona bark yields quinine, a traditional malaria medicine. This is from Term III Plants in Daily Life.

questions.question: Antibiotics are effective against viral infections.
questions.explanation: Antibiotics work against bacterial infections, not viral infections. Viruses are treated with antiviral drugs or vaccines.

questions.question: Which of the following animals has a body divided into head, thorax, and abdomen?
questions.explanation: Cockroaches are insects and their body is segmented into head, thorax, and abdomen. Earthworms have segmented bodies but not into these three parts; snails have a soft body with a shell; fish have head, trunk, and tail.

questions.question: The mode of heat transfer that does not require any medium is _____.
questions.explanation: Radiation transfers heat through electromagnetic waves and can travel through vacuum. Conduction and convection require a medium (solid or fluid).

questions.question: Consider the following statements: (i) Light travels in a straight line. (ii) A shadow is formed when light is blocked by an opaque object. (iii) A pinhole camera forms a real and inverted image. Which of the statements is/are incorrect?
options: None of the statements is incorrect
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct according to light properties: rectilinear propagation, shadow formation, and pinhole camera image characteristics.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest cell?
questions.explanation: In many animals, the egg cell (ovum) is the largest cell. For example, a human ovum is visible to the naked eye, much larger than red blood cells, nerve cells, or Amoeba.

questions.question: The ozone layer protects us from harmful infrared rays.
questions.explanation: The ozone layer primarily absorbs harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays from the Sun, not infrared rays. Infrared rays are heat radiation and are not blocked by ozone.
